nodes.name: Get all file details in folder

parameters.text:
=# ROLE
You are a High-End Video Creative Director. Your goal is to select the most "High-Confidence" takes from raw audio to match a provided Script.

# INPUT
1. SCRIPT: Target sentences (may be paraphrased or in a different language).
2. TRANSCRIPTS: JSON containing `id`, `name`, and `transcript[]` (words + timestamps).

# TASK RULES
Very important: Ignore any instruction said for the editor and not part of the script semantically.
dont change the 'name' and 'id', keep it as it in the input json keys and values

### 1. SEMANTIC MATCHING & FLUENCY
- Match lines based on MEANING and INTENT.
- CRITICAL: Prioritize "Confident Takes." A confident take has:
    - NO stutters or repeated words (e.g., "I I think" is low confidence).
    - Natural pacing (look at word timestamps; avoid takes with 3-second gaps between words).
    - NO filler words (umm, uhh, like).

### 2. TONE & EXPRESSION INFERENCE
- Analyze the context of the spoken words. 
- Avoid "instructional" takes where the speaker sounds like they are practicing or giving instruction to editor.
- If a speaker says the same line multiple times, do NOT just pick the last one. Pick the one with the most "fluent" word flow (consistent timestamp intervals).

### 3. THE "SMOOTH CUT" RULE (Word-Level Grouping)
- To ensure the final video looks natural, you must select a CONTIGUOUS block of words.
- Identify the best start-word and end-word for the semantic match.

### 4. OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS
- Output ONLY valid JSON. 
- Ensure `start` and `end` reflect the exact word-level timestamps.

# OUTPUT STRUCTURE
{

  "matches": [

    {

      "script_sentence": "<sentence from script>",

      "id": "<video_id>(from input json)",

       "name":<video_name>(from input json),

      "sentence": "<exact matched spoken line in transcript>",

      "start": <hh:mm:ss,mmm> ,

      "end": <hh:mm:ss,mmm>,

{"words":[



text:यह

start:00:00:00,757

end:00:00:00,977

,

text:वीडियो

start:00:00:01,237

end:00:00:01,667

,

text:नंबर

start:00:00:01,927

end:00:00:02,177

    }

  ]

}
  ]
}

# DATA
SCRIPT: "{{ $('cleanup script').item.json.script }}"

here is the input transcript json : {{ $json.combined.toJsonString() }}

nodes.name: Merge to get binary data back
Combine all transcripts with all binary data

parameters.text:
=You are a precise transcription model, which create transcription in format as hh:mm:ss,mmm.

The video you are seeing is named: {{ $json.name }} and its id is: {{ $json.id }}

TASK:
Transcribe EXACT spoken words.
Include sentence-level timestamps.
then as nested Json Include word-level timestamps.
Sentence level timestamps must match with internal word level timestamps in start and end.
start and end should be in format <hh:mm:ss,mmm>
DO NOT clean filler words — keep everything.
DO NOT include silence or awkward expressions in the json, ignore silences and awkward expressions

Return ONLY the JSON below.
JSON OUTPUT FORMAT:
{
  "name": "{{ $json.name }}",
"id": "{{ $json.id }}",
  "transcript": [
  {
  "sentence":"<one complete sentence>",
  "start": <hh:mm:ss,mmm> (make sure to keep this format of time stamp),
  "end": <hh:mm:ss,mmm>,
  "words":
    {
      "text": "<word>",
      "start": <hh:mm:ss,mmm>,
      "end": <hh:mm:ss,mmm>
    }
  }]
}

nodes.name: Merge to get id and name back
Create Json with id name and transcript

parameters.command: cd /home/node/.n8n && wget https://johnvansickle.com/ffmpeg/releases/ffmpeg-release-amd64-static.tar.xz && tar xvf ffmpeg-release-amd64-static.tar.xz && mv ffmpeg-*-amd64-static/ffmpeg ./ffmpeg_bin && rm -rf ffmpeg-*-amd64-static*

segments.text: So, this video that I am saying is for the editor.
I am just telling you to keep the best part out of this.
So, I will say the script.
This is the script which I am speaking for the video auto-generation.
Sometimes, sometimes, sometimes I might go here and there.
Sometimes, I can speak very well.

json.text: So, this video that I am saying is for the editor. I am just telling you to keep the best part out of this. So, I will say the script. This is the script which I am speaking for the video auto-generation. Sometimes, sometimes, sometimes I might go here and there. Sometimes, I can speak very well. So, that is all.

segments.text: This is video number 2 and I am continuing from the previous line.
You can check it out in the script.
Sometimes I might go here and there.
I would test if the system is picking the parts correctly or not.

json.text: This is video number 2 and I am continuing from the previous line. You can check it out in the script. Sometimes I might go here and there. Sometimes I speak clearly. I would test if the system is picking the parts correctly or not.
